hw-4 code

First i will load library and clean memory

# clean memory
rm(list = ls()) 

# location of the data
link="https://github.com/DACSS-Visual/tabular_multivar/raw/main/data/safeCities_ecoUI.xlsx"

# 'rio' can be used to import EXCEL files:
library(rio)
safeALL=import(link)

drop=c("Overall_Score")
keep=setdiff(names(safeALL),drop)
safe = safeALL[,keep]

str(safe,nchar.max = 60,strict.width='cut')
'data.frame':   60 obs. of  62 variables:
 $ city                                                : chr  "Abu Dhabi" "Amsterdam" "Baku" "Bangkok" ..
 $ D_In_PrivacyPolicy                                  : num  75 100 75 50 100 100 50 100 75 75 ...
 $ D_In_CitizenAwarenessOfDigitalThreats               : num  50 50 25 50 50 75 50 75 75 25 ...
 $ D_In_SecureSmartCities                              : num  100 50 50 100 50 50 50 50 50 50 ...
 $ D_In_CyberSecurityPreparedness                      : num  75 75 50 50 75 75 100 75 75 75 ...
 $ D_In_PublicprivatePartnerships                      : num  100 100 50 50 100 0 0 50 50 0 ...
 $ D_Out_PercentageWithInternetAccess                  : num  99.7 92.8 78.8 62.1 89.8 59.7 60.2 89.4 8..
 $ D_Out_SecureInternetServers                         : num  3.5 100 0.7 4.7 54.3 2.4 1 60.6 9.2 0.1 ...
 $ D_Out_RiskOfAttacks                                 : num  25 25 50 50 75 25 50 25 75 50 ...
 $ D_Out_ItInfrastructureRisk                          : num  100 100 50 50 75 75 75 100 50 50 ...
 $ D_Out_PercentageOfComputersInfectedFromOnlineAttacks: num  40 100 60 60 60 60 80 60 60 60 ...
 $ H_In_UniversalHealthcareCoverage                    : num  50 100 50 50 100 50 50 50 50 0 ...
 $ H_In_AccessToHealthcare                             : num  66.7 83.3 66.7 75 58.3 83.3 50 83.3 75 50..
 $ H_In_QualityOfHealthServices                        : num  87.5 75 75 62.5 75 62.5 37.5 75 75 37.5 ...
 $ H_In_HealthInfrastructure                           : num  48.5 37.7 46.5 24.4 43.7 59 19 45 52.6 6...
 $ H_In_AccessToSafeAndQualityFood                     : num  89.4 89.2 37.3 34.7 86.9 59 61.9 88.6 92 ..
 $ H_In_PolicyOnSubstanceAbuseDrugUse                  : num  100 100 0 100 100 100 100 0 100 0 ...
 $ H_In_PandemicPreparedness                           : num  37.3 89.6 24.1 87.7 57.7 43.4 34.2 73.8 5..
 $ H_In_MentalHealth                                   : num  100 0 0 100 100 100 0 0 0 100 ...
 $ H_Out_EmergencyServicesInTheCity                    : num  100 50 0 50 100 50 50 100 50 50 ...
 $ H_Out_LifeExpectancyYears                           : num  79.2 91.4 60.4 74.4 96.3 91 78 88.7 71.3 ..
 $ H_Out_InfantMortality                               : num  93.1 97.1 81.9 91.3 98.4 99.2 85.7 98.2 9..
 $ H_Out_CancerMortalityRate                           : num  97.2 65.1 75.2 69.5 75.9 51.6 79.3 69 66...
 $ H_Out_LifestyleRelatedDiseaseBurden                 : num  42.9 90.4 0 85.8 91.2 59.6 85.4 90.1 74.5..
 $ H_Out_MentalHealthBurden                            : num  55 26.4 89.1 84.7 16.6 92.2 75.1 42 55.5 ..
 $ H_Out_Covid19Mortality                              : num  97.2 91.6 93.7 100 70.6 99.9 64.2 97.1 97..
 $ I_In_EnforcementOfTransportSafety                   : num  100 60 80 60 80 80 50 70 50 70 ...
 $ I_In_PedestrianFriendliness                         : num  24.4 77.9 25.6 11.6 70.9 50 68.6 72.1 17...
 $ I_In_DisasterManagementbusinessContinuityPlan       : num  75 100 25 50 100 100 50 100 75 25 ...
 $ I_In_WaterInfrastructure                            : num  75 100 75 100 100 75 75 100 100 50 ...
 $ I_In_HazardMonitoring                               : num  100 100 100 100 100 100 50 100 100 50 ...
 $ I_Out_RoadTrafficDeaths                             : num  87.6 81.4 45.5 71 99.3 7.6 75.9 97.2 95.9..
 $ I_Out_DeathsFromClimaterelatedDisasters             : num  92 17.1 86.3 33.7 5.7 60 27.4 8 64 89.1 ...
 $ I_Out_TransportInfrastructure                       : num  58.3 91.7 50 41.7 91.7 83.3 33.3 83.3 50 ..
 $ I_Out_PowerNetwork                                  : num  100 100 75 50 75 75 75 75 25 100 ...
 $ I_Out_InstitutionalCapacityAndAccessToResources     : num  100 100 50 100 100 100 50 100 100 50 ...
 $ I_Out_CatastropheInsurance                          : num  50 50 50 100 50 50 100 50 0 0 ...
 $ I_Out_DisasterriskInformedDevelopment               : num  50 100 0 50 100 100 0 100 50 50 ...
 $ I_Out_LackOfBasicHousing                            : num  100 95.1 91.5 90.1 95.9 99.7 81.3 95.2 68..
 $ P_In_UseOfDatadrivenTechniquesForCrime              : num  100 100 50 50 50 100 50 100 50 50 ...
 $ P_In_GunRegulationAndEnforcement                    : num  54 75 70 58 74 83 68 69 75 58 ...
 $ P_In_ThreatOfViolence                               : num  80.6 91.7 72.2 38.9 80.6 77.8 38.9 91.7 7..
 $ P_In_CapacityOfTheJudicialSystem                    : num  45.3 61.8 18.6 24.1 60.6 56 61.2 62.8 31...
 $ P_In_ExpenditureOnSocialSecurity                    : num  16.1 51.6 3.2 12.9 80.6 3.2 41.9 93.5 6.5..
 $ P_In_LawsEnsuringWomensSafety                       : num  12.5 37.5 50 37.5 62.5 37.5 87.5 75 62.5 ..
 $ P_Out_PrevalenceOfCrime                             : num  100 87.5 50 62.5 87.5 62.5 12.5 75 50 62...
 $ P_Out_OrganisedCrime                                : num  75 100 50 50 75 50 25 75 50 75 ...
 $ P_Out_SeverityOfTerroristAttacks                    : num  97 83 92 35 77 48 40 71 89 31 ...
 $ P_Out_DeathsFromSubstanceUseDisorders               : num  67.3 87.4 80.7 85.2 77.5 90.1 98.5 69.4 8..
 $ P_Out_LevelOfCorruption                             : num  75 100 0 25 75 25 25 75 25 25 ...
 $ P_Out_EnforceabilityOfContracts                     : num  75 100 25 50 100 50 50 75 50 50 ...
 $ P_Out_EconomicSecurity                              : num  92.9 89.7 18.4 70.3 84.8 52.6 46.9 87.8 7..
 $ P_Out_WomensSafety                                  : num  78.9 83.9 88.5 69.6 91.3 97.8 52.9 82.3 6..
 $ E_In_SustainabilityMasterplan                       : num  100 100 100 50 100 50 100 100 100 50 ...
 $ E_In_IncentivesForRenewableEnergy                   : num  0 100 100 100 100 100 100 100 100 100 ...
 $ E_In_GreenEconomyInitiatives                        : num  100 100 0 100 100 100 100 100 100 50 ...
 $ E_In_WasteManagement                                : num  100 100 100 100 100 100 100 100 100 0 ...
 $ E_Out_SustainableEnergy                             : num  3.6 14.7 8.5 12.2 48 26.5 98.6 32.1 2.3 1..
 $ E_Out_RateOfWaterStress                             : num  0 100 0 25 25 0 75 0 100 0 ...
 $ E_Out_AirQualityLevels                              : num  25 75 0 75 75 25 75 75 75 75 ...
 $ E_Out_UrbanForestCover                              : num  0 37 0.7 9 62.8 26.9 26.9 36.8 26 0 ...
 $ E_Out_WasteGeneration                               : num  63.8 81.9 83 70.5 73.4 54.4 79.9 59.6 85...
library(reshape2)

safeL=melt(safe, # all the data
           id.vars = 'city') # unique id per row
head(safeL)

library(ggplot2)

safeL$type=ifelse(grepl("_In_",safeL$variable),'input','output')

safeL$variable=sub("_In_|_Out_",'_',safeL$variable)

# now we have:
head(safeL)

safeL$dimension=substr(safeL$variable, 1, 1)
safeL$variable=gsub("^[A-Z]_", "", safeL$variable)

#then
safeL|>head()
NA
NA
# Average by city and dimension
safe_avg = aggregate(value ~ city + type + dimension, 
                            data = safeL, 
                            FUN = mean, 
                            na.rm = TRUE)

# Check the result
head(safe_avg)

newLevels=c('DIGI','ENVIR','HEALTH','INFRA','PERS')
safe_avg$dimension=factor(safe_avg$dimension,
                          levels = c("D","E","H","I","P"),
                          labels = newLevels,ordered = F)

# different data frames
safe_avg_out=safe_avg[safe_avg$type=='output',]
safe_avg_in=safe_avg[safe_avg$type=='input',]
allIN=safe[,c(grep("_In_", names(safe) ))]
names(allIN)
 [1] "D_In_PrivacyPolicy"                            "D_In_CitizenAwarenessOfDigitalThreats"        
 [3] "D_In_SecureSmartCities"                        "D_In_CyberSecurityPreparedness"               
 [5] "D_In_PublicprivatePartnerships"                "H_In_UniversalHealthcareCoverage"             
 [7] "H_In_AccessToHealthcare"                       "H_In_QualityOfHealthServices"                 
 [9] "H_In_HealthInfrastructure"                     "H_In_AccessToSafeAndQualityFood"              
[11] "H_In_PolicyOnSubstanceAbuseDrugUse"            "H_In_PandemicPreparedness"                    
[13] "H_In_MentalHealth"                             "I_In_EnforcementOfTransportSafety"            
[15] "I_In_PedestrianFriendliness"                   "I_In_DisasterManagementbusinessContinuityPlan"
[17] "I_In_WaterInfrastructure"                      "I_In_HazardMonitoring"                        
[19] "P_In_UseOfDatadrivenTechniquesForCrime"        "P_In_GunRegulationAndEnforcement"             
[21] "P_In_ThreatOfViolence"                         "P_In_CapacityOfTheJudicialSystem"             
[23] "P_In_ExpenditureOnSocialSecurity"              "P_In_LawsEnsuringWomensSafety"                
[25] "E_In_SustainabilityMasterplan"                 "E_In_IncentivesForRenewableEnergy"            
[27] "E_In_GreenEconomyInitiatives"                  "E_In_WasteManagement"                         
rownames(allIN)=safe$city

##
head(allIN)

library(polycor)
library(psych)

#### notice you may need to install 'polycor' and 'psych'
## compute CM 
CM=polycor::hetcor(allIN)$correlations # 'hetcor' in case you have numerical and categorical

##CM is suitable
KMO_tested=psych::KMO(CM) 
##KMO measures how much the variables share common variance
##low values mean a variable is mostly noise
KMO_tested$MSA
[1] 0.7042502
# only the variables with a proper MSAi will remain
keep_vars = KMO_tested$MSAi >= 0.5
allIN_kmo_ok = allIN[, keep_vars]
ncol(allIN_kmo_ok)
[1] 23
## Detect the dimensions
CM_kmo_ok=polycor::hetcor(allIN_kmo_ok)$correlations
psych::fa.parallel(CM_kmo_ok, fm = "pa", fa = "fa",plot = F,n.obs = nrow(allIN_kmo_ok))
Parallel analysis suggests that the number of factors =  3  and the number of components =  NA 
#4. Compute the new dimensions
res_fa = psych::fa(CM_kmo_ok, n.obs = nrow(allIN_kmo_ok),
                    nfactors = 3, rotate = "varimax", fm = "pa")

print(res_fa$loadings)

Loadings:
                                              PA1    PA2    PA3   
D_In_PrivacyPolicy                             0.575  0.457  0.406
D_In_SecureSmartCities                                0.272  0.327
D_In_CyberSecurityPreparedness                 0.297  0.109  0.548
D_In_PublicprivatePartnerships                 0.311  0.129  0.320
H_In_UniversalHealthcareCoverage               0.637  0.191  0.179
H_In_AccessToHealthcare                        0.165  0.769  0.242
H_In_QualityOfHealthServices                   0.163  0.915  0.102
H_In_HealthInfrastructure                      0.370  0.617  0.247
H_In_AccessToSafeAndQualityFood                0.781  0.278  0.313
H_In_PandemicPreparedness                      0.370         0.692
I_In_EnforcementOfTransportSafety              0.236  0.461  0.304
I_In_PedestrianFriendliness                    0.597  0.532  0.102
I_In_DisasterManagementbusinessContinuityPlan  0.516  0.448  0.586
I_In_WaterInfrastructure                       0.645  0.364  0.341
I_In_HazardMonitoring                                 0.301  0.605
P_In_UseOfDatadrivenTechniquesForCrime         0.253  0.233       
P_In_ThreatOfViolence                          0.511  0.490  0.337
P_In_CapacityOfTheJudicialSystem               0.690        -0.166
P_In_ExpenditureOnSocialSecurity               0.711  0.195  0.280
P_In_LawsEnsuringWomensSafety                  0.397 -0.350  0.399
E_In_SustainabilityMasterplan                  0.463  0.235  0.460
E_In_GreenEconomyInitiatives                   0.438         0.254
E_In_WasteManagement                                  0.273  0.701

                 PA1   PA2   PA3
SS loadings    4.826 3.745 3.498
Proportion Var 0.210 0.163 0.152
Cumulative Var 0.210 0.373 0.525
city_scores = psych::factor.scores(allIN_kmo_ok, res_fa)$scores|>as.data.frame()
head(city_scores)

# normalizing
library(BBmisc)

efa_scores_norm=normalize(city_scores, 
                       method = "range", 
                       margin=2, # by column
                       range = c(0, 100))

# new  vars

city_scores$governance=efa_scores_norm[,1]
city_scores$health_system=efa_scores_norm[,2]
city_scores$preparedness=efa_scores_norm[,3]

city_scores[,4:6]
##reshaping
city_scores_wide=city_scores[,4:6]
city_scores_wide$city=rownames(city_scores_wide)
city_scores_long=melt(city_scores_wide,id.vars = 'city')

##plotting
baseefa=ggplot(data=city_scores_long) + theme_classic() 

varsefa=baseefa + aes(x=reorder(city, value,median),y=value)

geomsefa= varsefa + geom_point(shape=5, aes(color=value>75))+
                    geom_segment(aes(x=city,y=0,yend = value,xend = city),
                                 color='grey70',linewidth=0.2)
org_plot = geomsefa +  facet_grid(~variable) + coord_flip() +
            theme(axis.text.y = element_text(size = 10)) +
            labs(title = "Safety of Cities",
                 subtitle = "A 3-dimensional approach",
                 x="", y='Scores', color="Above 75 points")
org_plot

saveRDS(org_plot, file = "plot4.rds")

improved plot

city_scores = psych::factor.scores(allIN_kmo_ok, res_fa)$scores|>as.data.frame()
head(city_scores)

# normalizing
library(BBmisc)

efa_scores_norm=normalize(city_scores, 
                       method = "range", 
                       margin=2, # by column
                       range = c(0, 100))

# new  vars

city_scores$'Foundational Services/Governance' = efa_scores_norm[,1]
city_scores$'Healthcare Performance' = efa_scores_norm[,2]
city_scores$'Crisis Preparedness/Resilience' = efa_scores_norm[,3]

city_scores[,4:6]
##reshaping
city_scores_wide=city_scores[,4:6]
city_scores_wide$city=rownames(city_scores_wide)
city_scores_long=melt(city_scores_wide,id.vars = 'city')

##plotting
baseefa=ggplot(data=city_scores_long) + theme_classic() 

varsefa=baseefa + aes(x=reorder(city, value,median),y=value)

geomsefa= varsefa + geom_point(shape=21, color = "black", size = 2.5, stroke = .6, aes(fill=value>75))+
                    scale_fill_manual(
                        values = c("TRUE" = "black", "FALSE" = "white"),
                        labels = c("FALSE" = "Below 75", "TRUE" = "75 or higher"),
                        name = "Score Threshold"
                      ) +
                      geom_segment(aes(x=city,y=0,yend = value,xend = city),
                                 color='grey70',linewidth=0.2)
org_plot = geomsefa +  facet_grid(~variable) + coord_flip() +
            theme(axis.text.y = element_text(size = 10)) +
            labs(title = "Safety of Cities",
                 subtitle = "A 3-dimensional approach",
                 x="", y='Scores', color="Above 75 points")
org_plot

REORDER CITY


library(dplyr)
city_scores_wide = city_scores[,4:6]
city_scores_wide$city = rownames(city_scores_wide)

city_order <- city_scores_wide |>
  arrange(desc(`Foundational Services/Governance`)) |>
  pull(city)

city_scores_long = melt(
  city_scores_wide,
  id.vars = "city"
)

city_scores_long$city <- factor(city_scores_long$city, levels = rev(city_order))
baseefa = ggplot(data = city_scores_long) + theme_classic()

varsefa = baseefa + aes(x = city, y = value)

geomsefa = varsefa +
  geom_point(
    shape = 21,
    color = "black",
    size = 2.5,
    stroke = .8,
    aes(fill = value > 75)
  ) +
  scale_fill_manual(
    values = c("TRUE" = "black", "FALSE" = "white"),
    labels = c("FALSE" = "Below 75", "TRUE" = "75 or higher"),
    name = "Score Threshold"
  ) +
  geom_segment(
    aes(x = city, y = 0, yend = value, xend = city),
    color = "grey70",
    linewidth = 0.2
  ) +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 75, linetype = "dashed", color = "grey80") 
  

org_plot2 = geomsefa +
  facet_grid(~variable) +
  coord_flip() +
  theme(axis.text.y = element_text(size = 10)) +
  theme(legend.position = "none",
        plot.title = element_text(face = "bold")) +
  labs(
    title = "Cities Show Wide Variation Across Three EFA-Derived Dimensions",
    subtitle = "Strong performance in foundational services does not generally translate\nto healthcare or preparedness",
    x = "",
    y = "",
    caption = "Source: safeCities_ecoUI.xlsx\nScores are normalized to a 0–100 scale; filled points indicate values ≥75"
  )

org_plot2

saveRDS(org_plot2, file = "plot5.rds")
library(patchwork)

p4 <- readRDS("plot4.rds")
p5 <- readRDS("plot5.rds")

combined_plot <- p4 + p5 + plot_layout(ncol = 2)

saveRDS(combined_plot, "plot45.rds")
combined_plot
